food_subgroup: Dessert and beverage set meal — cheesecake slice with tall coffee or tea beverage

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002426 covers cheesecake as a food product; this record is a set meal combining a cheesecake slice with a tall beverage. The beverage component is not captured by this code. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKW covers cheesecake as a food category. The set meal nature (cheesecake + tall beverage) is not represented by a single FoodEx2 code. Closest parent used for the dominant solid component.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found that adequately represents a cheesecake and beverage set meal. Code left null.

main_ingredients: tall beverage (coffee or tea, milk-based or black)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one slice of New York cheesecake (approximately 130 g) paired with one Starbucks tall-size beverage (approximately 300 ml / 300 g), totalling approximately 430 g.

nutrient_source.title: Cheesecake, commercially prepared — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 18433); Beverage component estimated from USDA FoodData Central: Beverages, coffee, brewed, espresso, restaurant-prepared (NDB 14209) and whole milk (NDB 01077) for latte-style tall drink
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: The set meal combines a New York-style cheesecake slice and a tall beverage. Nutrient values per 100 g are derived by weighted combination: cheesecake slice ~130 g (USDA SR Legacy NDB 18433: cheesecake, commercially prepared) and tall latte-style beverage ~300 g (estimated from USDA FoodData Central whole milk and espresso components as a proxy for a standard Starbucks tall latte). Weighted average per 100 g of the combined 430 g serving is reported. This is an adapted estimate; exact Starbucks product formulation is proprietary.

gi_source.title: Cheesecake, plain — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 46 is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for plain cheesecake (Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care). The beverage component (tall latte-style coffee with milk) has a negligible independent GI contribution when combined with the cheesecake. The set meal GI is estimated as dominated by the cheesecake slice, which is the primary carbohydrate-contributing component. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (46 × 19.2 × 4.3) / 100 ≈ 16.8 g (using 430 g serving weight and 19.2 g carbohydrate per 100 g).

hypertension.guidance: Each serving of this set provides approximately 636 mg sodium (148 mg per 100 g × 4.3), which represents about 28% of the 2,300 mg daily sodium limit recommended for hypertensive individuals; individuals with hypertension should consume this set meal occasionally and monitor total daily sodium intake from other sources.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content and moderate glycemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a glycemic index of 46 (low) but a glycemic load of approximately 16.8 per serving and approximately 62 g total sugar per set meal, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit this set to occasional consumption and pair it with a low-carbohydrate meal to manage postprandial blood glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.guidance: One serving of this set meal provides approximately 24 g saturated fat (5.6 g per 100 g × 4.3), exceeding the American Heart Association's recommended daily limit of 13 g saturated fat for a 2,000 kcal diet; individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat this as an occasional indulgence and offset with low-saturated-fat choices throughout the day.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Good Ol' NY Cheesecake with Tall Drink Set is a dessert-and-beverage combo served at Starbucks Singapore, available from 2 PM daily. It pairs a classic New York-style baked cheesecake slice with a customer's choice of tall-size (12 fl oz / 355 ml) Starbucks beverage. The New York cheesecake is characterised by its dense, rich, and creamy texture derived from a high proportion of full-fat cream cheese, eggs, and sour cream, set on a buttery digestive biscuit or graham cracker base. The flavour profile is tangy, sweet, and indulgent with a smooth mouthfeel. Nutritionally, the set is energy-dense, providing approximately 752 kcal per serving, with significant contributions from fat (particularly saturated fat from cream cheese and butter) and sugar. The glycemic index of the cheesecake component is approximately 46 (low GI), but the glycemic load per serving is moderate at around 16.8 due to the portion size. The set is relevant in clinical nutrition contexts because of its high saturated fat content (relevant to cardiovascular disease risk), moderate sodium (relevant to hypertension), and moderate glycemic load (relevant to Type 2 Diabetes management). It is vegetarian and halal-certified at Starbucks Singapore. The beverage component varies by customer choice and may include lattes, cappuccinos, teas, or Frappuccinos, which can substantially alter the total calorie, sugar, and caffeine content of the set.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Starbucks NY cheesecake set?
Is the Starbucks cheesecake set healthy?
What is the GI of New York cheesecake?
Good Ol NY Cheesecake Starbucks nutrition

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for commercially prepared cheesecake and a proxy latte-style beverage, weighted by serving size. The exact Starbucks Singapore product formulation is proprietary and not publicly available in any approved primary source. GI is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database via Atkinson et al. 2008 for plain cheesecake as the closest analogous food. Confidence is medium due to the adapted nature of the nutrient derivation and the variable beverage component.
notes: 1. This record represents a set meal (cheesecake slice + tall beverage) sold at Starbucks Singapore via field survey. The beverage component is variable by customer choice; nutrient values assume a standard tall latte (whole milk) as the representative beverage for calculation purposes. 2. Nutrient values per 100 g are a weighted average of the combined 430 g serving (130 g cheesecake + 300 g tall latte). 3. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data and are approximate. 4. GI of 46 is from the University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al. 2008) for plain cheesecake; this is an analogous estimate for the set meal. 5. The price noted in the input ($14.90 usual, $14.00 on field survey) is preserved as contextual information only and does not affect nutritional scoring. 6. Halal status reflects Starbucks Singapore's general halal certification; individual beverage choices may vary. 7. All -1 values in per_100g represent nutrients not independently reported for this adapted composite estimate in the USDA source; they are set to -1 per sentinel value convention — however in this record all micronutrients have been estimated from component data and no field remains at -1.

Verified all core macros non-zero. Verified ontology match_type values are within allowed set (exact, closest_parent, none). Verified no -1 sentinel values remain undocumented.
